title: Stocks Fall as Chip Selloff Hits Kospi, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
summary: Semiconductor stocks tumbled across Asia, dragging regional equities lower, as investors became more skeptical that the artificial intelligence-driven rally can withstand lofty valuations. Oil slipped.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil steadied after a three-day rally as the US carried out another round of airstrikes on Iran following a series of shipping attacks by the Islamic Republic this week.

title: Syngenta’s $5 Billion Hong Kong IPO Is Said to Face Fresh Delays
summary: Syngenta Group’s planned $5 billion Hong Kong initial public offering is facing delays as the seed and pesticide company waits for better conditions in the agriculture sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Asian Gas Prices Rise to Highest Since March on Mideast Tensions
summary: Asian liquefied natural gas prices rose to the highest since late March as fresh hostilities in the Middle East deepened concerns that shipping through the key Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted for longer.

title: China Seeks to Curb New Bond Sales by LGFVs With Increased Debt
summary: A major Chinese bond regulator is taking fresh steps to curb debt sales by the nation’s municipal borrowers, according to people familiar with the matter, adding pressure on weaker issuers looking to refinance notes.

title: Gold Declines as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Fuel Inflation Fears
summary: Gold declined, erasing a moderate gain from the previous session, as escalating tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns that energy prices and inflation will stay high.

title: Wheat Holds Near Two-Month High After Black Sea Attacks
summary: Wheat futures held gains after jumping 5% on Wednesday, as Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea threatened a key export route for the warring countries.

title: Global Investors Hunt For Creative Backdoors Into China’s Mega CXMT IPO
summary: Global investors largely shut out of China’s biggest IPO in nearly four years are looking at creative proxy trades as a way to profit from the country’s fast-rising memory-chip leader.

title: Korea Plans Measures on Leveraged ETFs Driving Wild Volatility
summary: South Korea will soon announce measures regarding the leveraged exchange-traded funds tied to shares of Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. that have fueled a surge in market volatility.

title: Adani Flagship Draws Mutual Fund Interest as Governance Risks Recede
summary: Local mutual funds bought Adani Enterprises shares worth 46.60 billion rupees last month.

title: Pound Index Hits One-Year High on Mahmood for Chancellor Reports
summary: A measure of sterling’s strength rose to its highest level in a year on Wednesday following reports that incoming UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham will name Shabana Mahmood as his Chancellor of the Exchequer.

title: BHP Posts Solid Full-Year Output, Sees Copper Decline Ahead
summary: BHP Group Ltd. posted solid full-year production despite a weaker final quarter, though it warned copper output will fall in the year ahead as grades decline at its flagship Chilean mines.

title: India Raises Export Levies on Diesel and Jet Fuel
summary: India increased taxes on exports of ‌diesel and aviation turbine fuel as the collapse of the US-Iran interim peace deal and renewed hostilities have choked oil flows again tightening global fuel markets.

title: Modi Pushes to Cut Import Reliance to Shield India From Shocks
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is taking steps to reduce key imports into the economy in order to protect supply chains and ease pressure on the currency as geopolitical risks escalate.

title: Japan PE Fund Seeks $1.9 Billion Economic-Security Investments
summary: Advantage Partners LLC plans to invest in big domestic companies posing economic security risks for Japan that might benefit from government backing and boost profits in the long term.

title: Kalshi Seeks to Let Traders Bet on Flight Cancellation Rates
summary: Your flight? Canceled. The silver lining? You might be able to make some money off the misery.

title: IEA Boss Warns Global Economy in Peril If Hormuz Crisis Persists
summary: The global economy faces a renewed challenge if the conflict that’s choked the Strait of Hormuz isn’t resolved in a matter of weeks, said International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol.

title: BHP Iron Ore Workers Stage First Port Hedland Strike Since 2000
summary: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland operations in Western Australia will stage the first strike at the company’s Pilbara iron ore hub since 2000, after last-ditch talks failed to avert industrial action.

title: Liquidity in Malaysia May Tighten Further as Rate-Hike Bets Grow
summary: Funding conditions in Malaysia’s money market will likely tighten further later this year, with some analysts expecting stronger economic growth to prompt the country’s central bank to raise interest rates.

title: Iran War Drives New Investment in US LNG, S&P Global Says
summary: The Iran war, and its disruptions across global energy markets, is spurring investments for new US liquefied natural gas export infrastructure, according to consulting firm S&P Global.
